raw_response:
I agree with the assessment provided by agent1. Gram-negative rods such as *Porphyromonas gingivalis* and *Prevotella intermedia*, which fall into category B, have been well-documented for their initial colonization capabilities because they efficiently use available nutrients even under fluctuating oxygen levels typical near teeth surfaces. Additionally, these organisms produce adhesins and other virulence factors aiding them in establishing themselves quickly against environmental challenges posed during plaque formation.
